body_txt: A veteran detective is haunted by that case they could never solve. Alternately, perhaps it was "solved" officially, but the detective thinks something doesn't add up, and thus the true perpetrator remains at large. Either way, this failure still gnaws at the detective. Cases like this are almost always about a Serial Killer (or at least an especially gruesome murder) and/or something personal . Even when the detective is officially off the clock, they might still work on the case in their free time, because it is that important to them. If they are told to leave the case alone they may get fired over it , but even when it comes to that, they'll still persist despite that they are not a cop any more . Sub-Trope of My Greatest Failure . Compare Revisiting the Cold Case . Truth in Television : A number of real life homicide detectives have solved cold cases that were haunting them, or are haunted by the still unsolved ones.
